content:
First note that 7, 11 and 13 are pairwise coprime, so working mod 1001=7·11·13 we have

“n² has the same remainder r mod 7, 11 and 13”  
⇐⇒ n²≡r (mod 7), n²≡r (mod 11), n²≡r (mod 13),

and since r mod 7 must lie in {0,…,6}, necessarily r∈{0,1,2,3,4,5,6}.  By the Chinese remainder theorem the number of solutions mod 1001 for a given r is

 e₇(r)·e₁₁(r)·e₁₃(r),

where e_p(r)=1 if r≡0 (mod p), =2 if r is a nonzero quadratic residue mod p, and =0 otherwise.  One checks easily that among r=0,…,6 only r=0,1,4 give nonzero contributions:

r=0: e₇=e₁₁=e₁₃=1 ⇒1 solution;  
r=1: e’s are all 2 ⇒2·2·2=8 solutions;  
r=4: e’s are all 2 ⇒8 solutions.

Hence there are 1+8+8=17 solutions in each full block of length 1001.  Since 2017=2·1001+15, there are

 2·17=34 solutions in 1≤n≤2002

and one must add those n with 2003≤n≤2017 (i.e. residues 1,…,15 mod 1001) which again satisfy the common‐remainder condition.  A quick check of n=1,…,15 shows only n=1,2 work, giving 2 more.  Altogether

 34+2=36.

Answer:  \boxed{36}.

message.content:
First note that 7, 11 and 13 are pairwise coprime, so working mod 1001=7·11·13 we have

“n² has the same remainder r mod 7, 11 and 13”  
⇐⇒ n²≡r (mod 7), n²≡r (mod 11), n²≡r (mod 13),

and since r mod 7 must lie in {0,…,6}, necessarily r∈{0,1,2,3,4,5,6}.  By the Chinese remainder theorem the number of solutions mod 1001 for a given r is

 e₇(r)·e₁₁(r)·e₁₃(r),

where e_p(r)=1 if r≡0 (mod p), =2 if r is a nonzero quadratic residue mod p, and =0 otherwise.  One checks easily that among r=0,…,6 only r=0,1,4 give nonzero contributions:

r=0: e₇=e₁₁=e₁₃=1 ⇒1 solution;  
r=1: e’s are all 2 ⇒2·2·2=8 solutions;  
r=4: e’s are all 2 ⇒8 solutions.

Hence there are 1+8+8=17 solutions in each full block of length 1001.  Since 2017=2·1001+15, there are

 2·17=34 solutions in 1≤n≤2002

and one must add those n with 2003≤n≤2017 (i.e. residues 1,…,15 mod 1001) which again satisfy the common‐remainder condition.  A quick check of n=1,…,15 shows only n=1,2 work, giving 2 more.  Altogether

 34+2=36.

Answer:  \boxed{36}.
